purpose: Molecular taste data for Aureon consciousness sensory integration — gustatory channel
integration_notes: Each molecule is sequenced via MolecularSequencer in aureon_taste_sense.py
Taste score maps to the emotional frequency codex (Reason 400Hz → Illumination 800Hz)
Receptor Kd values are T1R2/T1R3 heterodimer estimates from published binding studies
Sweetness potency is relative to sucrose (sucrose = 1×)

molecules.name: Sucralose
molecules.notes: Chlorinated sucrose derivative; passes through body mostly unmetabolised; marketed as Splenda

molecules.name: Aspartame
molecules.notes: Dipeptide methyl ester; phenylalanine + aspartic acid; heat-unstable; common in diet beverages

molecules.name: Saccharin
molecules.notes: Oldest artificial sweetener (1879); sulfonamide ring; slight metallic aftertaste at high concentrations

molecules.name: Rebaudioside-A
molecules.notes: Steviol glycoside from Stevia rebaudiana; clean sweetness profile; minimal bitterness vs stevioside

molecules.name: Acesulfame-K
molecules.notes: Oxathiazinone dioxide potassium salt; often blended with aspartame for synergistic sweetness; heat-stable

molecules.name: Cyclamate
molecules.notes: Sodium cyclohexylsulfamate; lowest potency in set; banned in US (1969) but approved in EU and Canada; used in blends

molecules.name: Neotame
molecules.notes: N-neohexyl aspartame derivative; 10,000× sucrose; does not require phenylketonuria warning; extremely potent

molecules.name: Advantame
molecules.notes: Aspartame-vanillin derivative; highest known potency at ~20,000× sucrose; FDA approved 2014; ultra-trace usage

frequency_map.description: Maps composite taste_score [0.0–1.0] to emotional frequency spectrum

sensory_node.name: TASTE
sensory_node.description: Molecular gustatory resonance channel — chemical binding translated to emotional frequency
